dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, the situation has turned worse. Much worse.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {enemy_type} didn't wait for us to finish our defenses. They attacked the outer farms last night. Three families lost their homes. {target_npc} moved the refugees deeper into the settlement, but the council is furious. They're drafting an exile order. I have until dawn to convince them otherwise, but words won't do it: I need proof we can hold this place. Clear the {enemy_type} camp {location_hint} and bring back evidence that we've weakened their numbers. If the council sees we can fight, maybe {quest_stakes} won't come to pass.
dialogueChunks.outro: Dawn. That's all I have. If I can't show the council a reason to hope by then, my friend and fifty refugees walk out those gates into certain death.

playerResponses.text: I'll hit their camp tonight. You'll have your proof by morning.
Racing against the council and the enemy at the same time. Just another day.
The council may be right. Sometimes the many outweigh the few.

dialogueChunks.intro: I need you to listen carefully, {player_name}. The purification plan hit a wall.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The materials you brought can filter sediment but not the poison itself. The contamination is magical in nature: some residue from the {enemy_type} that nested near the water table. {target_npc} found an old alchemical text that describes a neutralizing agent, but it requires {quest_item} from deep underground {location_hint}. Worse, {target_npc}'s family heard about our plans and they're threatening to seal the cavern. They think we're trying to take their water by force. I need you to gather the reagent AND speak to {target_npc} before their family does something irreversible.
dialogueChunks.outro: If they seal that cavern, {quest_threat} destroys two communities instead of one. Fear makes people do terrible things.

playerResponses.text: I'll talk to {target_npc} first, then get the reagent. Trust between neighbors matters.
Politics and poison. What a combination. I'll handle both.
I'm not a diplomat. You need someone who can negotiate, not fight.

dialogueChunks.intro: The truce deadline moved up, {player_name}. They're coming tomorrow.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: A second {enemy_type} warband joined the first. Their demands doubled: now they want the eastern AND western harvest stores. That's not three families going hungry, that's the entire settlement. {target_npc} says we have to fight, but fighting means guards die: guards with families of their own. My sister sent word that she'd rather starve than see soldiers killed protecting her grain. I need you to gather more {quest_item} from {location_hint} and I need you to do it faster than you've ever done anything. If we can stockpile enough before they arrive, we hand over the stores and nobody starves.
dialogueChunks.outro: My sister volunteered to starve. My soldiers volunteered to die. I won't accept either offer. Help me refuse them both.

playerResponses.text: Nobody starves and nobody dies. I'll make it happen.
Doubled demands. Of course. I'll move fast.
You can't outrun a warband with a basket of supplies. This is futile.
